title: haplopappus uniflorus subsp. howellii
intro: Haplopappus uniflorus subsp. howellii, also known as Howell's golden ragwort, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Haplopappus uniflorus subsp. howellii include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by drying and crushing the leaves to use as a topical ointment or tincture.
